content:
Binary output issue
* According to the contribution guidelines, binary outputs should be avoided for clashes. That's exactly the issue with your clash, as it allows only two possible answers (either "YES" or "NO"). Please modify the output requirement to allow for more than two possible answers.

Stub generator
* Please change Line 2 to use `loopline`, e.g. loopline N S:int.

Input
* Please use the variable name [[S]] (or another name used in the stub generator) to refer to each water lily position, and specify that [[S]] is an integer rather than a string.

Constraints
* Please add the lower bound for [[S]].

Cases
* Please renumber the cases so that the case numbers are in ascending order.

* Please remove all trailing newlines from the input.

Thanks.

Very neat concept; I like it! Here is some feedback:
1. Output: Fix the typo "Ligne 1" to "Line 1"
2. Statement: State that the frog's position starts at 0

That's all I have; some other reviewers might give you more feedback
Thank you for your feedback and the corrections I made to the statement.

(also to @j3r3m) I think an idea to avoid binary outputs is to print the 1-based index of the water lily that the frog stopped on. I also noticed how your test cases don't cover the maximum boundary of [[n]], which is 10, so try adding a test case that covers this maximum boundary. Also, I recommend maybe slightly increasing it as 10 "feels" a bit too small.
